description: Responsive dashboard layout with mobile sidebar toggle, dynamic user info from session, proper logout, and improved header. The sidebar currently has hardcoded 'Lanchonete do Joao' and is fixed at w-64 with no mobile support (ml-64 on content means content is hidden on mobile). DashboardHeader already reads session but sidebar does not.
steps: 1. Refactor src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx: Make it responsive - add mobile hamburger toggle, use a collapsible overlay on mobile (hidden by default, shown via state). Replace hardcoded 'Lanchonete do Joao' / 'LJ' initials with dynamic session data using useSession() from next-auth/react. Show actual user name and company name from session. Add logout button at the bottom.

2. Refactor src/app/[locale]/(dashboard)/layout.tsx: Make it a client component that manages sidebar open/close state. On mobile, remove ml-64 and show a hamburger button. On desktop, keep the sidebar visible. Pass toggle state to Sidebar component.

3. Refactor src/components/layout/DashboardHeader.tsx: Add a mobile hamburger menu button (visible only on mobile/md breakpoint) that triggers the sidebar. Improve the dropdown menu with settings/profile links. Ensure the user menu properly closes when clicking outside (add click-outside handler).
4. Add smooth transitions/animations to sidebar open/close on mobile (translate-x transition, backdrop overlay).
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar shows actual user name from session instead of hardcoded text
On mobile viewports (< 768px), sidebar is hidden by default and togglable via hamburger button
Logout button in sidebar works and redirects to login page
Content area is fully visible on mobile without ml-64 offset
User menu dropdown in header closes when clicking outside
verification: Run npx tsc --noEmit in /projects/sandbox/YUMYLAND to verify TypeScript compiles
Visually inspect that the code changes are syntactically correct and follow existing patterns
findings: node_modules directory does not exist in the workspace, so npx tsc --noEmit produces 2217 pre-existing errors across all files (Cannot find module 'react', 'next', etc.). All errors on modified files are the same generic missing-module errors affecting all project files. Code follows existing patterns exactly. Use NODE_OPTIONS='' prefix when running npx commands to avoid proxy-bootstrap.js error.
